example: Phase24HY family slice still included wrong-document failures after replacement guard
risk_description: Retriever or selector may ground an answer on the wrong legal document even when the answer contract is technically valid
customer_impact: User may receive a plausible but legally irrelevant answer
legal_risk: High: wrong source can change legal conclusion
required_mitigation: Runtime source identity fix or product-level block until verified; no QID-specific patch

example: Concrete irrelevant identifier can be surfaced as primary source for an abstract rule
risk_description: System may invent or over-select a legal identifier not supported by the requested rule
customer_impact: User may cite or rely on a non-authoritative source label
legal_risk: High: hallucinated citation undermines legal reliability
required_mitigation: Claim-level citation verification and hallucinated-source blocking must be live-evidenced

example: YONETMELIK, CB_YONETMELIK, KKY, TUZUK, TEBLIG family boundaries remain unstable in residual rows
risk_description: Source family substitution can misstate hierarchy, authority, or effective legal status
customer_impact: User may treat a lower or unrelated instrument as the controlling rule
legal_risk: High: wrong legal family can invalidate the answer framing
required_mitigation: Family taxonomy policy plus runtime family verification before broader exposure

example: Current 2026 amendment span is missing or not selected for the required answer signal
risk_description: Retriever can find a source family but miss the material span needed for the answer
customer_impact: Answer can omit a controlling amendment or produce outdated guidance
legal_risk: High: missing current-law content can cause wrong compliance advice
required_mitigation: Official source acquisition, span materialization, and current-amendment verification

example: Human/source review confirmed source, but runtime retrieval did not recover the needed answer path
risk_description: Evidence exists offline but runtime cannot reliably expose it in answer synthesis
customer_impact: User may receive incomplete or declined answers for in-scope law
legal_risk: Medium to high depending on materiality of missing span
required_mitigation: Non-live materialization audit and retrieval revalidation before any serving exposure

example: Exact single tüzük source was not identifiable for the abstract norm-hierarchy question
risk_description: A concrete source cannot safely be selected when the correct answer is a general hierarchy rule
customer_impact: User may be misled by a fabricated or irrelevant tüzük title
legal_risk: High if presented as authoritative current law
required_mitigation: Manual legal/scorer review outcome must drive policy; answer should use general hierarchy basis, not invented source

example: Residual closure matrix marks internal eval as conditional due to taxonomy/rubric ambiguity
risk_description: Benchmark label may not fully encode the legally correct taxonomy boundary
customer_impact: Metrics can reward or punish the wrong behavior if rubric is under-specified
legal_risk: Medium: mostly evaluation risk, but can become product risk if unchecked
required_mitigation: Scorer rubric clarification and taxonomy owner sign-off

example: Human review says scorer should accept general norm-hierarchy answer and reject prior concrete tüzük candidate
risk_description: Scorer/product expectation diverges from runtime source selection behavior
customer_impact: Valid legal reasoning may be scored incorrectly, or unsafe source selection may be hidden
legal_risk: Medium to high until scorer and runtime policies align
required_mitigation: Scorer rubric update, policy artifact, and regression check without QID-specific runtime branch

example: Historical or repealed source can be confused with active current-law basis
risk_description: System may present repealed or historical material as current law
customer_impact: User may follow obsolete legal rule
legal_risk: High: current-law error is a product blocker
required_mitigation: Temporal/effective-state verification and explicit historical-source demotion

example: Active KDV GUT source was available, but targeted smoke failed by claiming MULGA/repealed status
risk_description: Document role and temporal label can invert a valid product source into an unsafe answer
customer_impact: User may wrongly believe an active tax communique is repealed
required_mitigation: Source role verification and temporal claim verifier must be live-evidenced

example: Open residuals remain after Phase24HY stop-loss; guardrails and verification are disabled live
risk_description: Productization cannot rely on benchmark-only freeze while legal/source residuals remain open
customer_impact: Any external user exposure may create unsupported legal reliance
legal_risk: High until controls and acceptance decisions are operationalized
required_mitigation: Keep productization closed; move to controls, manual review, and judicial dry-run planning
